description: Use reconciliation against source of truth before trusting balances or reports.
body: Derived balances and reports should reconcile with ledger/source events. Use when finance engineering work touches money movement, ledger entries, reconciliation output, idempotency keys, audit trails, and approval records. Do not use this guidance to override a more specific pack, user instruction, or repository contract. Actions: choose the smallest direction that preserves the domain contract; inspect the current files first, keep the change scoped to the owning boundary, and record what evidence changed the decision. Validation signals include decimal precision tests, duplicate-submit tests, reconciliation reports, immutable audit evidence, and human approval notes. Risk boundary: escalate or stop when the task involves silent balance changes, floating-point money, missing audit trails, regulatory ambiguity, and irreversible financial state, missing owner evidence, or live external state.
